summary: Pristine front‑of‑house audience tape captures a solid second set highlighted by a smoking Scarlet > Fire, while the first set feels uneven after an early on‑stage hiccup.
review: The recording is a superb FOB audience capture with tight bass, clear mids and just enough crowd ambience to feel present. Musically the show starts shaky – an early “Half‑Step” incident leaves the first set disjointed – but the second half fires on all pistons, delivering standout versions of Scarlet > Fire and Sugar Magnolia along with strong drumming and Phil’s buoyant bass work.

ai_rating.rationale: Excellent recording quality boosts the rating, but the performance is mixed – a weak first set offset by an outstanding second set.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Fully‑saturated front‑of‑house tape recorded near the PA boxes; slightly thin high end but tight bass and clear mids. Crowd is present as ambience only, no individual voices.

standout_songs: Scarlet Begonias > Fire On The Mountain
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set feels disorganized after an on‑stage mishap, but the band regroups for a tight, high‑energy second set that maintains momentum and showcases strong interplay.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Early solo was drawn out due to a stage issue; later his playing steadies as the second set builds.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bouncing all around with buoyant bass lines, especially in the second set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Amazing drums, loud and driving throughout; highlighted by reviewers.
